channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Trump heads to China for high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping
item.description: The US president will meet with Xi Jinping in a visit that could reshape superpower relations amid Iran war tensions and trade negotiations. Middle powers fear Trump might trade security commitments for better economic terms.

item.title: Pakistan airstrike killed 269 Afghans at rehab centre, families demand answers
item.description: A March 16 Pakistani airstrike on what officials called a militant target killed 269 Afghans at a rehabilitation centre. The UN says the death toll may be higher, with calls mounting to investigate the strike as a war crime.

item.title: EU imposes sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
item.description: The European Union agreed to sanction Israeli settlers accused of violence against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank. Israel called the move "arbitrary" while the EU foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences."

item.title: Duterte drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest in Philippines
item.description: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, former national police chief under ex-President Duterte, has sought refuge in the Philippine senate to avoid arrest by the International Criminal Court over his role in the deadly drug war.

item.title: Israel passes law allowing death penalty for October 7 detainees
item.description: Israel approved legislation permitting public trials and death penalty for those involved in the October 7 Hamas-led attacks. Rights groups warn the law strips fair trial protections and makes executions easier to impose.

item.title: Zelensky's former chief of staff Yermak charged in major corruption probe
item.description: Ukraine's anti-corruption authorities have charged Andriy Yermak, former head of the presidential administration and Zelensky's closest aide, with corruption offenses. The case involves state-owned nuclear energy company Energoatom and marks the highest-level corruption investigation since the war began.

item.title: UAE secretly carried out strikes on Iranian oil facilities in April
item.description: The UAE covertly attacked Iranian oil infrastructure on Lavan Island in the Persian Gulf in early April, according to US media reports. The UAE had been a target of Iranian retaliation but had not publicly acknowledged conducting offensive operations against Iran.

item.title: US says Pakistan allowed Iran to park military aircraft on its airbases
item.description: A US report claims Pakistan permitted Iran to station military aircraft on Pakistani territory during the conflict. The allegation could strain US-Pakistan relations and raises questions about regional alignments during the Iran crisis.

item.title: France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea amid Hormuz crisis
item.description: France has deployed its aircraft carrier to the Red Sea, positioning it for potential operations near the Strait of Hormuz as the US-Iran conflict continues. The move underscores European concerns about energy security and freedom of navigation.

item.title: Israel Passes Death Penalty Law for October 7-Linked Defendants
item.description: Israel Knesset passes law permitting death penalty and public trials for those involved in the Hamas-led October 7 attacks, a major shift in Israeli judicial policy.

item.title: Duterte Drug War Enforcer Evades ICC Arrest in Philippines
item.description: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, former national police chief under Duterte, sought refuge in the Philippine senate to avoid arrest warrant from the International Criminal Court.

item.title: EU Agrees Sanctions on Israeli West Bank Settlers
item.description: The European Union reached agreement to sanction Israeli settlers over West Bank violence after Hungary new government dropped its veto. Israel branded the move "arbitrary".

item.title: Asia Braces for Second Wave of Energy Shocks from Iran War
item.description: Countries across Asia are preparing for another round of energy disruptions as the Iran conflict continues to threaten oil and gas supplies through critical waterways.

item.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte Impeached for Second Time
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a second impeachment complaint, which if convicted in a Senate trial would disqualify her from running for president in future elections.

item.title: Canada Seeks Defense Partnership With Turkey as Middle Powers Align
item.description: Canada is rapidly expanding defense ties with Turkey, viewing Ankara as a trusted middle-power partner for co-development of drones, counter-drone systems, and ammunition amid shifting global security dynamics and US retrenchment.

item.title: Sweden Detains Two Suspects for Aiding High-Tech Shipments to Russia
item.description: Swedish authorities arrested a Swedish and a Turkish national on suspicion of violating EU sanctions by helping Russia procure advanced technology products that could be used in the war against Ukraine.

item.title: Russia Paid North Korea Up to $13.8 Billion for War Support, South Korean Intel Says
item.description: North Korea has earned between $7-13.8 billion supplying weapons including artillery and ballistic missiles to Russia, with an estimated 6,000 North Korean casualties in Ukraine and plans to send 30,000 more soldiers.

item.title: UK Sanctions 85 People and Entities Over Russian Information Warfare
item.description: Britain imposed new sanctions targeting Russian propaganda operations including attempts to interfere in Armenian elections, alongside measures against those involved in the forced deportation and indoctrination of Ukrainian children.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire on "Life Support" After Rejecting Tehran Peace Proposal
item.description: President Trump dismissed Iran's counter-offer to end the war as "garbage," saying the ceasefire was on "massive life support." Iran demanded sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and war reparations while rejecting any removal of its enriched uranium.

item.title: South Africa's Ramaphosa Refuses to Resign Despite Impeachment Push
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he will legally challenge a report paving the way for impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala farm scandal. He vowed to fight the findings which alleged serious misconduct.

item.title: Trump-Xi Summit Could Define Superpower Relations for Years, BBC Analysis Says
item.description: The first US presidential visit to China in nearly 10 years comes as Trump focuses on Iran, military operations, and domestic concerns. Taiwan, trade tensions, and technology competition are at stake, with both leaders seeking cooperation on the Iran conflict.

item.title: China Passenger Car Exports Surge 85% in April as Domestic Sales Slump
item.description: China exported 796,000 vehicles in April, up 85% year-on-year, with EV exports jumping 120%. Domestic sales fell 25.5% amid reduced subsidies and economic uncertainty. Analysts expect continued export growth despite trade tensions.

item.title: Trump Announces Plan to Suspend Federal Gas Tax Amid Soaring Fuel Prices
item.description: President Trump said he would suspend the 18-cent federal gasoline tax to offset prices that have risen 50% since the Iran war began. The move requires congressional approval, with Senator Hawley planning legislation. Gas prices now average $4.52 per gallon.

item.title: Trump Nominates Kari Lake as US Ambassador to Jamaica
item.description: Former Arizona news anchor and Trump ally Kari Lake has been nominated as ambassador to Jamaica. Lake recently led efforts to dismantle Voice of America, terminating hundreds of employees. She thanked Trump, saying Jamaica "is a country I know very well."

item.title: Nearly 200 Sanctioned Russian Ships Entered UK Waters Despite Warning
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows 184 UK-sanctioned "shadow fleet" vessels made 238 journeys through UK waters since March, with no public evidence of boardings. Critics call lack of action "pathetic" amid legal constraints.
